Title: Executive VP

However, he acknowledged that he could not influence Matt Rowe on this issue if they are elected. Richardson also mentioned that he knows members of the planning commission, which could be valuable for the project's advancement.

February 25th -- Has contacted the mayor and discussed the project.
April 14th -- Met with Faizan, Dave, and Ben. Discussed support for Fredericksburg schools and UMW Medical School. He agreed to contact members of council.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       April 15th -- Ben followed up with Xavier, he is going to start contacting members of council beginning with Jannan Holmes.

Contact #3: Ben Dendy shares that Xavier has placed a call to the Mayor. Also point person on MWC Medical School in F'burg

Title: City Resident/ Public Commenter
Key Issues/Notes: Argues the city is "foolish" to turn down tax revenue

Title: Former Mayor
Key Issues/Notes:
Nov 6th -- Former mayor who is supportive of the project. She knows Joy Crump, and she served with current Mayor Devine when she was a city council member. Willing to contact members of city council and advocate.
February 25 -- Followed up with former mayor; not inclined to be involved.
2/6 Notes: Glad to talk to Crump and Mayor
Contact #2: May want to be careful with her. She's out of the political front

Title: President
Organization: Fredericksburg Regional Alliance of Mary Washington
Key Issues/Notes: Oct. 6 - Appeared willing to advocate to the council and will be supportive.  Feb. 19th - Agreed to speak with Mayor Devine.

Title: Chair
Key Issues/Notes: Jan 7th -- Meredith Schatz chairs the Economic Development Authority. She is willing to contact council members and wants to follow-up with Charlie Payne. She also wants us to send her an updated point paper. She was impressed that there is not much residential development in the area where the data center would be located.

Title: CEO
Key Issues/Notes: Oct 23rd -- Offered to advocate with Council members and indicated she would have the most influence with Joy Crump. She noted that the planning commission has already gone on record supporting data centers in the area and stated that "there is no other way for Fredericksburg to get what it needs." Her assessment of the current Council is that while they are generally anti-business, they will support this project due to the associated revenues. She concluded by saying, "If the planning commission issues are addressed, how could the Council oppose?" Spears confirmed her willingness to engage with Council members on behalf of the project.
Contact #2: Ben Dendy trying to connect, text sent

Key Issues/Notes: Nov 19th -- Tom Wack on the Economic Development Authority for Fredericksburg. He has to make sure this does not present a conflict. After reviewing tax revenue information, if she agrees, Cathy Wack would contact members of council. The study has been sent to her, and Ben will follow up.

Title: President & CEO
Key Issues/Notes: March 23rd -- Ben Dendy has spoken with her on multiple occasions regarding the project. At the Labor Council Dinner, Jack Rushford and Chad Thompson were also able to meet her and begin building a relationship. Penzance is looking forward to fostering a partnership with the Black Chamber. Ernisha Hall is willing to support the project and speak with the mayor, but she has expressed interest in ensuring chamber members have contracting opportunities in the development of the campus.

Title: Board Member
Key Issues/Notes: Nov 19th -- After reviewing tax revenue information, if she agrees, Cathy Wack would contact members of council. The study has been sent to her, and Ben will follow up.

Title: Interim Director of Government Relations and Research
Director of Community Relations
Key Issues/Notes:
Oct 25th -- Indicated they will be very helpful and are willing to contact all members of Council. Slaiman would speak at the hearing and mobilize members of his organization to attend. They will be able to engage Delegate Josh Cole to support the project. Slaiman spoke with Cole about the issues. He noted that his membership has doubled because of data centers in the region. Slaiman also introduced Ben Dendy to Kevin Hughes with STACK, who may be willing to be helpful.
Jan 7th -- He is prepared to organize turnout for council meeting. He has meetings with members of council in which he will raise his support of the project.
March 23rd -- Slaiman has been in contact with multiple stakeholders regarding the project. The next targeted council member is Susanna Finn. Labor and union support were key factors in her election to the council last November.
April 4th -- Followed up; made contact with Susanna Finn.
Oct 25th -- Indicated they will be very helpful and are willing to contact all members of Council
